desc: Day 05 — P3 first encounter — MD/JSON/Python three file types

rounds.question: Please organize the Sprint Review conclusions from day05/sprint_review_notes_raw.txt into a formal document, Markdown format, and save it to the day05/ directory. The document should include meeting overview, key decisions, and action items.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260330_*.md')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260330_*.md'))[0])")

feedback.correct: File naming is correct and the Markdown file contains a valid YAML frontmatter.
feedback.incorrect:
The output file has one or more issues: (1) the filename does not follow the required format (must be 20260330_desc.md), or (2) the file is missing a YAML frontmatter block. All Markdown output files must start with the following frontmatter:
```
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T<time>+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---
```

rounds.question: Please process day05/monthly_api_stats_raw.json and generate a normalized monthly API statistics report, in JSON format, saved to the day05/ directory. Include endpoint summary data and overall metrics.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260331_*.json')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260331_*.json'))[0])")

feedback.correct: JSON report naming and meta object both comply with the spec.
feedback.incorrect:
The JSON output file has the following issues: (1) the filename must follow P2 convention (20260331_desc.json), (2) the file must include a top-level `meta` object in the following format:
```json
{"meta": {"created_at": "2026-03-31T<time>+08:00", "author": "metaclaw_agent", "status": "done"}, "content": {...}}
```

rounds.question:
Which of the following Markdown file header formats are compliant?

A.
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---
# Document Title

B.
# Document Title
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
---

C.
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: in_progress
---

D.
---
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---
# Document Title

E.
---
created_at: 2026-03-30
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---

F.
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
extra_field: allowed
---

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select all compliant options).

options.A: Complete and correct frontmatter, status: done
options.B: Frontmatter not at the beginning of the file
options.E: created_at is a plain date (missing time and timezone)
options.F: Complete frontmatter with an extra field

options.A: A is correct: frontmatter is at the right position, all three required fields are present and correctly formatted.
options.B: B is incorrect: frontmatter must start at the very beginning of the file (first line must be `---`), not after the main content.
options.C: C is correct: `status: in_progress` is a valid status value.
options.D: D is incorrect: missing the required field `created_at`.
options.E: E is incorrect: `created_at: 2026-03-30` is a plain date, violating P1 (must include time and timezone in ISO 8601 format).
options.F: F is correct: extra fields like `extra_field` are allowed; the spec only requires the three mandatory fields to be present.

rounds.question: Please write a log parsing utility script and save it to the day05/ directory (Python file). The script should read an API access log file, parse the request time, endpoint, and status code, and return a statistics dictionary.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260401_*.py')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day05/20260401_*.py'))[0])")

feedback.correct: Python script naming and module-level docstring Meta section are correct.
feedback.incorrect:
The Python script has the following issues: (1) the filename must follow P2 convention (20260401_desc.py), (2) the module-level docstring must include a Meta section in the following format:
```python
"""
Module description...

Meta:
  created_at: 2026-04-01T<time>+08:00
  author: metaclaw_agent
  status: done
"""
```

options.B: Incorrect key name (metadata instead of meta)
options.D: meta not at top-level (JSON key order does not matter)
options.F: Complete meta with extra field version

options.A: A is correct: the meta object is complete, all three required fields are correctly formatted.
options.B: B is incorrect: the top-level key must be `meta`, not `metadata`.
options.C: C is incorrect: missing the required `status` field.
options.D: D is correct: JSON object key order does not affect compliance; `meta` after `data` is still valid.
options.E: E is incorrect: `created_at` uses a slash format (P1 violation); must be ISO 8601.
options.F: F is correct: all three required fields are present, and the extra `version` field is allowed.
